_comment: Exception list for scripts/audit-mutation-coverage.mjs. Each entry is { file, function, reason }. Functions listed here may emit DML without a sibling recordAudit() call.

exceptions.reason: Public marketing form, pre-tenant; tracked in public.contact_submission. Recorded by /contact/admin/* triage actions instead.
Public newsletter confirmation; pre-tenant — no audit context.
Read-only triage listing (no DML).
Lifecycle table sales.status_history INSERT is itself an audit/history record; recordAudit emits status_change separately.
Idempotent auto-escalation; assignment + activity inserts inside withTx are themselves the audit trail (ops.case_activity).
recordAudit is emitted inside withTx after all DML completes.
recordAudit is emitted inside withTx after all DML completes (status_change OR update branch).
Wave 1 — covered by Phase 1 (P-010) backfill, not Sprint 0.
Wave 1 — covered by Phase 1 (P-010) backfill, not Sprint 0.
Phase 2 — GIS audit is tracked separately in crm-plan-map.md.
Analytics rollup writes are non-business mutations; tracked in analytics rollup_log.
Platform-admin triage; outbound transitions tracked via the contact_submission status column itself.
Migration runner; bootstraps the audit_event table itself — emit-on-self would be circular.
Email body composition with embedded SQL keywords in literal strings, not DML.
Anonymous pre-auth invite consumption — audit emitted by /auth/register-with-invite once tenant attribution is known.
Background maintenance — drops naturally-expired revocation rows on a 15-minute timer; not a business mutation, no tenant context.
Sprint 2A — the activity row IS the timeline/audit mirror; callers pair every recordAudit() with an emitActivity() call. Emitting recordAudit from inside the emitter would be circular.
S3B — pref upsert helper; the calling REST handler in iam/email_prefs.mjs emits the recordAudit pair.
S3B — pref upsert helper; the calling REST handler in iam/email_prefs.mjs emits the recordAudit pair.

S3B — notify dispatchers call enqueue() (local helper) and recordAudit() explicitly; legacy direct-send functions write audit rows through the audit() helper, which the coverage walker cannot see across the call boundary.

S3B — background maintenance that re-arms outbox rows stuck in 'sending' past the claim TTL (process-crash recovery). Not a business mutation; the subsequent drainOnce() emits audit rows on terminal state changes.
S4A — provisions a per-tenant data encryption key on first use. Internal KEK helper, not a business mutation; the calling /calendar credential mint flow emits the recordAudit pair.
S4A — DEK rotation routine. Writes per-credential rotation_log rows which are the audit trail; recordAudit is emitted by the calling /iam/keys/rotate REST handler.

S7C — visibility-aware READ helper (SELECT-only). The audit-coverage scanner false-positives on the case-insensitive word 'update' appearing in a comment elsewhere in the file (line 169, getLeadById doc). No DML in this function.

Sprint A5.2 — district aggregate UPSERT helper (analytics.org_rollup, aggregates only, no PII). The calling REST handler refresh() emits recordAudit({action:'org.rollup.refresh'}); per ADR-0019 a real deployment moves this to the transactional outbox.

S10B — pre-tenant access-code verification. The function inlines an INSERT INTO iam.audit_event (action='access.verify.success') because recordAudit() requires req.tenant.id which is unavailable on this anonymous endpoint. The inline INSERT is the audit. Failure attempts are journaled to stderr — there is no tenant attribution for invalid codes by definition.
